name: Precision
satire: Lands the blow that matters — clean pulls, no fumbled rotation.

name: Instinct
satire: Reads the fight a beat early — out of the fire before it blooms.
effect: Reduces damage taken, with a little extra output.

name: Composure
satire: Holds the line when it all goes sideways — one death never becomes five.
effect: Clutch output and survival while the party is in danger, and steadier (less swingy) results overall.
